constraints: One warren run per child seed, each bound to this Plot via plot_id so the activity feed accumulates the coordination
No per-child version bump — bundle into a single warren 0.4.5 housekeeping release at the end (warren-c106 shipped as 0.4.3; warren-ede7 shipped as 0.4.4)
Quality gates (bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck) green per child
Each child closes its corresponding seed via 'sd close' at completion; this Plot only auto-transitions to done when all 9 close
intent.goal: Close 9 ambient open seeds (1 high-pri infra bug, 1 robustness, 7 small bug/polish items) so the warren backlog reflects current ambition, not accumulated drag. Validate Plot as a coordination object for a multi-run unit of work — this is the first Plot dogfood after the v0.3.18 / v0.4.0 / v0.4.1 / v0.4.2 Plot infrastructure releases.
non_goals: The pg dialect repo-layer refactor (warren-0f34) — substantial enough to be its own Plot
The Plot commit-through-reap follow-up (warren-343a) — its own Plot when ready
success_criteria: All 9 listed seeds closed: warren-0375, warren-b1a9, warren-c666, warren-ab18, warren-6596, warren-9b15, warren-b3aa, warren-44e3, warren-44a3
One warren 0.4.5 patch release bundles them with a single CHANGELOG entry summarizing the housekeeping pass
Plot detail page shows the full coordination at done — every run_dispatched event visible, every PR linked via artifact_produced, intent + substrate + activity feed all coherent
At least one concrete Plot-UX learning recorded as an ml insight (or filed as a follow-up seed) that wasn't visible before dogfooding

name: Housekeeping pass — burn down ambient bugs + polish before next epic
